metadata.description: Canonical structural backbone spanning appendicular, axial, neurovascular, developmental, imaging, and surgical anatomy.
Canonical shoulder-to-hand regional anatomy module.
Canonical pelvis-to-foot regional anatomy module.
Canonical cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, neural, and stabilizing anatomy module.
Canonical osseous, ligamentous, muscular, and neurovascular pelvic anatomy module.
Canonical peripheral nerve, plexus, arterial, venous, and vascular-territory module.
Canonical intervals, internervous planes, safe zones, and surface landmarks.
Canonical physes, ossification centers, and age-dependent skeletal anatomy.
Seven mobile vertebrae supporting the head and protecting the cervical spinal cord.
Twelve rib-bearing vertebrae forming the thoracic spinal column.
Five load-bearing vertebrae between the thoracic spine and sacrum.
Fused sacral vertebrae transmitting axial load to the pelvic ring.
Anterior load-bearing component of a vertebra.
Bridge between vertebral body and posterior elements; key screw corridor.
Posterior element forming the dorsal wall of the spinal canal.
Paired zygapophyseal articulation guiding segmental motion.
Fibrocartilaginous motion-segment structure comprising annulus and nucleus.
Lamellar outer portion of an intervertebral disc.
Gelatinous central portion of an intervertebral disc.
Osseoligamentous canal containing the spinal cord and cauda equina.
Central neural structure extending from the brainstem to the conus medullaris.
Tapered caudal end of the spinal cord.
Lumbar and sacral nerve roots descending below the conus.
Root leaving through the corresponding neural foramen.
Root crossing the lateral recess before exiting at the next level.
Longitudinal stabilizer along anterior vertebral bodies.
Longitudinal stabilizer along posterior vertebral bodies.
Elastic ligament connecting adjacent laminae.
Artery traversing cervical transverse foramina toward the posterior circulation.
C5-T1 neural network supplying the upper extremity.
Lumbar and sacral neural network supplying the pelvis and lower extremity.
Anterior thigh interval containing femoral nerve, artery, vein, and lymphatics.
Posterior knee space containing tibial nerve and popliteal vessels.
Volar fibro-osseous canal containing median nerve and flexor tendons.
Posteromedial elbow tunnel transmitting the ulnar nerve.

metadata.description: Plantarflexor compartment containing gastrocnemius and soleus.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Deep Posterior Compartment of Leg

metadata.description: Deep flexor compartment containing tibial nerve and posterior tibial vessels.
Fascial compartment containing thenar musculature.
Central deep hand fascial compartment.
Anterior shoulder interval between deltoid and pectoralis major.
Volar forearm approach plane using radial and median nerve territories.
Lateral elbow interval between anconeus and extensor carpi ulnaris.
Lateral elbow interval between extensor digitorum communis and extensor carpi radialis brevis.
Anterior hip interval between sartorius and tensor fasciae latae.
Anterolateral hip interval between tensor fasciae latae and gluteus medius.
Common knee arthrotomy along the medial patella and quadriceps tendon.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Radial Nerve Humeral Safe Zone

metadata.description: Operative corridor defined by predictable radial nerve relationships to the humerus.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Common Fibular Nerve Safe Zone

metadata.description: Operative corridor protecting the common fibular nerve around the fibular neck.
Space transmitting axillary nerve and posterior circumflex humeral artery.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Femoral Head Blood Supply

metadata.description: Predominantly retinacular perfusion from the medial femoral circumflex artery.
Anastomotic talar perfusion vulnerable to neck displacement.
Predominantly retrograde dorsal carpal perfusion of the proximal pole.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Humeral Head Blood Supply

metadata.description: Circumflex humeral arterial contributions to the proximal humerus.
Cartilaginous growth plate between epiphysis and metaphysis.
End segment of a developing long bone adjacent to the physis.
Flared long-bone segment between physis and diaphysis.
Initial diaphyseal site of endochondral ossification.
Epiphyseal or apophyseal site of postnatal ossification.
Capitellum, radial head, medial epicondyle, trochlea, olecranon, and lateral epicondyle sequence.
Y-shaped acetabular growth cartilage joining ilium, ischium, and pubis.
Hyaline cartilage covering synovial joint surfaces.
Bone plate and trabecular region immediately deep to articular cartilage.
Fibrous and synovial enclosure of a synovial joint.
Muscle and soft-tissue vectors that predict displacement of fractures or reconstructions.
Palpable landmarks used for examination, imaging localization, and surgical access.
Canonical radiographic, CT, MRI, and ultrasound landmarks.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Upper Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite shoulder-to-elbow model linking clavicle, proximal humerus, shaft, distal humerus, and neurovascular structures.
S-shaped strut connecting sternum and acromion; common fracture site in shoulder trauma.
Lateral clavicle articulation with acromion; stability relevant in clavicle and shoulder injuries.
Medial clavicle articulation with manubrium; posterior displacement can threaten mediastinal structures.
Humeral head, neck, and tuberosity region forming the shoulder articular unit.
Articular surface of the proximal humerus in the glenohumeral joint.
Metaphyseal region distal to humeral head where proximal humerus fractures commonly occur.
Circumflex nerve at risk in shoulder trauma and proximal humerus surgery.
Humeral diaphysis between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Posterior spiral groove nerve at risk in humeral shaft and distal humerus trauma.
Major arm artery coursing anteriorly; critical in supracondylar vascular exams.
Distal metaphyseal and articular humerus forming the proximal elbow.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Medial Column Distal Humerus

metadata.description: Medial distal humeral column including trochlear region and medial epicondylar support.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Lateral Column Distal Humerus

metadata.description: Lateral distal humeral column including capitellar articular support.
Posterior distal humerus recess accommodating olecranon in extension.
Lateral distal humeral articular surface for radial head articulation.
Medial distal humeral articular surface for ulnar articulation.
Motor branch of median nerve tested by FPL/FDP index function; injured in supracondylar fractures.
Anterior elbow and forearm nerve at risk in distal humerus and supracondylar injuries.
Posteromedial elbow nerve at risk in distal humerus and elbow trauma.
Hinge joint between distal humerus, radius, and ulna.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Primary knee stabilizer commonly injured with tibial plateau and knee trauma.
Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Hand & Wrist Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite forearm-to-fingertip model linking radius, ulna, carpus, metacarpals, digits, tendons, ligaments, nerves, and vessels for Prepare Hand rotation.
Lateral forearm bone forming the radial side of the wrist and radiocarpal articulation.
Medial forearm bone with distal ulnar head and styloid contributing to DRUJ stability.
Forearm longitudinal fibrous linkage transmitting axial load between radius and ulna.
Distal radioulnar joint stabilizers and ulnar-sided wrist linkage.
Proximal radioulnar joint enabling forearm rotation.
Distal radial metaphysis and articular surface at the wrist.
Ulnar head and styloid region at the ulnar wrist.
Radial-sided carpal bone with retrograde blood supply and snuffbox tenderness landmark.
Central proximal carpal bone in perilunate and lunate dislocation patterns.
Ulnar-sided proximal carpal bone articulating with the lunate and TFCC.
Sesamoid bone within FCU tendon at the ulnar wrist.
Radial-sided distal carpal bone articulating with the thumb metacarpal.
Distal carpal bone between trapezium and capitate.
Central distal carpal bone linking proximal and distal carpal rows.
Ulnar-sided distal carpal bone with hook for flexor tendon passage.
Thumb metacarpal articulating with trapezium at the CMC joint.
Index metacarpal with stable CMC articulation.
Middle finger metacarpal forming the central hand column.
Ring finger metacarpal.
Small finger metacarpal; common site of boxer fractures.
Proximal segment of each digit articulating at MCP and PIP.
Middle segment of digits 2–5 between PIP and DIP.
Terminal phalangeal segment bearing the nail bed.
Distal interphalangeal articulation of digits 2–5 and IP of the thumb.
Proximal interphalangeal articulation governing finger flexion cascade.
Metacarpophalangeal articulation linking metacarpal to proximal phalanx.
Carpometacarpal articulation; thumb CMC is saddle-shaped and highly mobile.
Intrinsic ligament between scaphoid and lunate; SL injury drives carpal instability.
Intrinsic ligament between lunate and triquetrum; LT injury contributes to ulnar-sided instability.
Triangular fibrocartilage complex stabilizing the ulnar wrist and DRUJ.
Ulnar collateral ligament of the thumb MCP joint; injured in skier/gamekeeper thumb.
Fibrocartilaginous volar restraint at PIP preventing hyperextension.
Radial and ulnar collateral ligaments stabilizing MCP and IP joints.
Flexor pollicis longus tendon flexing the thumb IP joint.
Flexor digitorum profundus tendons flexing DIP joints; zone II no-man's land.
Flexor digitorum superficialis tendons flexing PIP joints.
Extensor pollicis longus tendon extending the thumb IP joint.
Extensor pollicis brevis tendon extending the thumb MCP joint.
Abductor pollicis longus tendon abducting and extending the thumb.
Extensor digitorum communis tendons extending MCP and IP joints.
Extensor carpi ulnaris tendon depressor and ulnar deviator at the wrist.
Flexor carpi radialis tendon; volar wrist approach interval landmark.
Flexor carpi ulnaris tendon inserting on pisiform and stabilizing ulnar wrist.
Flexor tendon pulley A1 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A2 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A3 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A4 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A5 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley C1 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley C2 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley C3 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Annular (A1–A5) and cruciate (C1–C3) pulleys preventing bowstringing of flexor tendons.
Zone I–V flexor tendon anatomic divisions governing repair strategy and prognosis.
Zone I–VIII extensor tendon anatomic divisions governing repair and splinting strategy.
Anterior interosseous nerve branch of median nerve; motor to FPL and index FDP.
Posterior interosseous nerve branch of radial nerve at the proximal forearm.
Proper digital nerves supplying sensibility to the fingers.
Radial artery at the wrist forming part of the deep arch.
Ulnar artery at Guyon canal contributing to palmar arches.
Palmar arterial arcade primarily from ulnar artery.
Deep palmar arterial arcade primarily from radial artery.
Ulnar tunnel at the wrist containing ulnar nerve and artery.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Sports Medicine Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite sports medicine model linking knee, shoulder, elbow, and foot-ankle regional hubs.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Sports Knee Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Knee ligament, meniscus, extensor, and articular structures for sports knee conditions.
Primary medial knee stabilizer resisting valgus stress.
Primary lateral knee stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Posterolateral knee stabilizing complex including LCL, popliteus, and arcuate ligaments.
C-shaped medial meniscus distributing load and resisting shear at the medial compartment.
More mobile lateral meniscus with popliteus hiatus; commonly torn with ACL injuries.
Medial and lateral femoral condyles forming the proximal tibiofemoral articulation.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Sports Shoulder Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Glenohumeral stabilizers, rotator cuff, and biceps anchor for shoulder sports conditions.
Scapular articular surface forming the socket of the glenohumeral joint.
Fibrocartilaginous rim deepening the glenoid and anchoring capsulolabral structures.
Supraspinatus, infraspinatus, subscapularis, and teres minor force-couple complex.
Superior rotator cuff tendon initiating abduction and commonly torn in cuff pathology.
Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.
Anterior cuff tendon providing internal rotation and anterior stability.
Inferior posterior cuff muscle contributing to external rotation.
Superior labrum and biceps anchor complex at the glenoid origin.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Sports Elbow Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Elbow ligament and tendon structures relevant to throwing and flexion injuries.
Medial elbow stabilizer critical in throwing athletes.
Lateral elbow stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Distal biceps insertion on the radial tuberosity enabling elbow flexion and supination.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Sports Foot & Ankle Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Lateral ankle ligaments, syndesmosis, Achilles, and hindfoot structures for sports foot-ankle conditions.
Primary lateral ankle stabilizer injured in inversion sprains.
Lateral ankle ligament stabilizing subtalar motion after inversion injury.
Conjoined gastrocnemius-soleus tendon inserting on the calcaneus.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Proximal Femur Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite proximal femur model linking head, neck, trochanters, calcar, and hip joint.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
Extracapsular metaphyseal zone between neck and subtrochanteric diaphysis.
Medial proximal femur attachment for iliopsoas; key subtrochanteric landmark.
Lateral proximal femur with abductor insertions; intertrochanteric fixation landmark.
Dense medial femoral neck cortical buttress supporting head-neck load transfer.

description: Create anatomy_structure: Medial Femoral Circumflex Artery

metadata.description: Dominant retinacular blood supply to the femoral head; vulnerable in neck fractures.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.
Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.

payload.claim_text: The spinal cord usually terminates as the conus near L1-L2 in adults; level variation must be respected during neuraxial and spine procedures.
The medial femoral circumflex retinacular system is the dominant blood supply to the adult femoral head.
Talar perfusion enters largely through the neck and body; displaced neck injuries threaten the body blood supply.
The scaphoid proximal pole depends predominantly on retrograde intraosseous flow from dorsal vessels.
The physis is mechanically weaker than adjacent pediatric ligaments and is vulnerable to injury.
The cephalic vein marks the deltopectoral interval and may be mobilized medially or laterally.
Forearm pronation moves the posterior interosseous nerve away from the lateral elbow operative field.
The common fibular nerve must be identified and protected where it wraps around the fibular neck.
Predictable muscle vectors explain fracture displacement and guide reduction maneuvers.
Articular cartilage restoration requires attention to joint congruity and the supporting subchondral bone.
